search.quickCompareNote: Search by name, area, or curriculum tag. This is a quick comparison tool — always confirm the latest fee sheet and availability with admissions.
search.placeholderCompare: Type a school name (e.g., Nature School, Canggu, Montessori)
search.tipCompareToggle: Tip: you can also add schools from the directory using the “Compare” toggle.

guidanceForm.schoolHelp: If you’re a school operator, use this form to request updates, add photos, or ask about verified or featured options.
guidanceForm.parentHelp: Tell us your area, child age, curriculum preference, and budget. We’ll help you shortlist and draft questions for admissions.
guidanceForm.schoolNotesPlaceholder: Examples: updated fee sheet, admissions dates, photo gallery, verified badge request…
guidanceForm.parentNotesPlaceholder: Anything that matters (language support, commute limits, learning support, start date)…
guidanceForm.weDoNotStore: We don’t store submissions on this website yet. The message opens in WhatsApp so you can review it before sending.
guidanceForm.messageOpensWhatsApp: The message opens in WhatsApp so you can review it before sending.

parentFeedback.intro: Share a short note that helps other families. Please keep it anonymous (no full names, no identifying details).
parentFeedback.notePlaceholder: Example: The teachers were warm and communication was consistent. Our child settled in quickly and loved the outdoor learning time.
parentFeedback.tip: Tip: Specific notes are most helpful (communication, learning approach, community, transitions, support).
parentFeedback.consent: I confirm this note is truthful and I’m happy for it to be published anonymously.
parentFeedback.noServerSubmit: This form does not submit to a server. It opens a WhatsApp message so you can review it before sending.

home.feesCardFeeNotesBody: See which schools list fees publicly and which ranges are best-effort estimates.
home.feesCardMethodologyBody: Read our methodology for fees, curriculum tags, parent perspectives, and sponsored placements.

school.interestHelp: Interested in this school? Use our central interest form and we will capture your inquiry with the school already prefilled.
school.parentPerspectivesIntro: These anonymized parent perspectives are intended to help families prepare questions for a tour or admissions conversation.
school.parentPerspectivesEmpty: We’re still collecting parent perspectives for this school.
school.photosPlaceholderNote: Photos on this page are placeholders. Replace them with school-provided images when available.
school.areasFamiliesAlsoConsiderIntro: These areas appear often among similar schools. Use them as quick shortcuts while you’re shortlisting.
school.claimUpgradeIntro: School operator? You can claim this page to update fees, admissions notes, and photos. Verified and featured options are available and will be clearly labeled.

schoolsPage.heroSubtitle: Search the directory by area, curriculum, and budget.
schoolsPage.browseBudgetIntro: If you’re early in the process, start with a budget band, then confirm total first-year cost with admissions.
schoolsPage.browseAgeIntro: Use broad age bands to narrow the list quickly, then compare curriculum and fees.
schoolsPage.browseTypeIntro: International, bilingual, Montessori, and more — use type as a quick starting point.
schoolsPage.compareToolIntro: Build a short list and compare fees, ages, and curriculum tags side-by-side.
schoolsPage.feesGuideIntro: Tuition is only part of the picture. Learn what to ask so you can compare schools fairly.
schoolsPage.feesDisclaimer: Fees are shown as best-effort annual ranges. Please confirm directly with each school.

faq.q1: How do we shortlist schools in Bali?
faq.a1: Start with commute/area, then match age range and curriculum. After that, compare total first-year cost and ask about admissions steps and availability.
faq.a2: Fees are best-effort annual ranges. Schools can update fees at any time, so always request the latest fee sheet and confirm what’s included.
faq.q3: What should we ask during a school tour?
faq.a3: Ask about class sizes, language support, learning support options, start dates, and what fees include (registration, uniforms, transport, meals, exams).
faq.q4: Can you help us choose a shortlist?
faq.a4: Yes. Share your area, your child’s age, and a couple of preferences and we’ll suggest a shortlist plus a simple question list to send to admissions.

parentFeedbackPage.heroSubtitleLead: Your note will be published anonymously on the profile for
parentFeedbackPage.guidelineOne: Keep it anonymous (no names, no phone numbers, no personal identifiers).
parentFeedbackPage.guidelineThree: Specific is best: communication, community, learning approach, transitions, support.

claimPage.heroSubtitleTail: and ask about verified and featured options.
claimPage.itemAdmissions: Admissions steps, start dates, and waitlist notes
claimPage.itemVerifiedFeatured: Optional verified badge and featured placement (clearly labeled)
claimPage.contactIntro: The fastest way is WhatsApp. Please message from an official school number or include an official email.

posts.directoryShortcutsBody: Quick links based on this guide. Use these pages for up-to-date fees, ages, and admissions notes.

posts.browseDirectoryFallback: Browse the directory for the latest profiles.

guides.title: Guides
guides.hubSubtitle: Practical guides for choosing a school in Bali — fees, areas, curriculums, and admissions.

typesPage.title: School types
typesPage.subtitle: Browse schools by type, then open profiles to confirm curriculum, ages, and fees.

combine.title: Combine type with area
combine.body: Type helps you narrow quickly, but commute is often the deciding factor. Pick an area first, then check type.

fees.title: Fees & budget
fees.body: Compare schools using total first‑year cost. Budget bands are the fastest way to filter early.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: What does a school type mean?
faq.a1: Type labels are a simple browsing shortcut. Schools sometimes use different wording, so always open a profile and confirm the exact program, language of instruction, and age/grade coverage.
faq.q2: Is ‘international school’ always the best option?
faq.a2: Not necessarily. Many families prioritize commute, community fit, and support. Use type as a starting point, then shortlist based on age range, curriculum pathway, and total first‑year cost.
faq.q3: How should we shortlist schools after choosing a type?
faq.a3: Pick your area first (traffic matters), then confirm ages/grade levels and curriculum. Finally, compare fees by asking for the full first‑year total (tuition + one‑time fees + recurring extras).
faq.a4: Yes. If you share your area, child’s age, and a couple of priorities (curriculum, budget, language support), we’ll suggest a shortlist and the exact questions to send to admissions.

comparePage.title: Compare schools
comparePage.subtitle: Pick a few schools and compare fees, ages, curriculum tags, and area — then jump into profiles for details.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: What’s the best way to compare schools fairly?
faq.a1: Use the same question list for each school. Compare commute, age/grade coverage, curriculum pathway, and total first-year cost (tuition + one-time fees + recurring extras).
faq.q2: Are the fees on this site exact?
faq.a2: Fees are best-effort ranges. Schools can update pricing at any time. Always request the latest fee sheet and confirm what’s included.
faq.q3: How many schools should we shortlist?
faq.a3: Most families shortlist 3–5, then do tours close together so the comparisons are fresh.
faq.a4: Yes. Share your area, your child’s age, and a couple of priorities (curriculum, language support, budget) and we’ll help you shortlist and draft admissions questions.

area.title: Popular area comparisons
area.body: If you’re deciding where to live, these comparisons help you understand commute and school options.

ages.title: Browse by age
ages.body: Prefer to start with age/grade coverage? Use broad age bands, then narrow by area and curriculum.

meta.title: {typeName} schools in Bali
meta.description: Browse {typeName} options in Bali. Compare areas, ages, curriculum tags, and fee ranges, then open school profiles for admissions notes.

hero.title: {typeName} schools in Bali
hero.feeRange: Typical annual ranges span roughly Rp {minFee}–Rp {maxFee}.

areas.title: Where you’ll find these schools
areas.empty: Area tags aren’t available for these listings.

curriculums.title: Common curriculum tags
curriculums.empty: Curriculum tags aren’t listed for these schools.

budgets.title: Budget bands
budgets.empty: Budget bands aren’t available for these listings.

nextSteps.title: Next steps
nextSteps.body: Once you’ve shortlisted by commute and age range, open 3–5 profiles and compare the full first-year cost.

faq.a1: Type labels are a browsing shortcut. Schools sometimes use different wording, so confirm the curriculum, language of instruction, and grade coverage directly with admissions.
faq.a2: Start with commute/area, then confirm ages/grades and curriculum pathway. Finally compare total first-year cost so quotes are apples-to-apples.
faq.q3: Should we browse by type or use the directory filters first?
faq.a3: Use type labels for quick browsing, then switch to the directory if you want to filter by multiple criteria (area + curriculum + budget).
faq.q4: Can you help us choose between a few schools?
faq.a4: Yes. Share your area, child’s age, and a couple of priorities and we’ll help you shortlist and draft the admissions questions to send.

moreFilters.title: Want to filter further?
moreFilters.body: Use the directory to combine area + curriculum + budget filters.

shortlist.title: Need a shortlist?
shortlist.body: Tell us your child’s age, your area, and a rough budget. We’ll suggest a shortlist and a question list for tours.

areaDetail.a1: This page lists schools tagged in {areaName}. Open a school profile to check ages, curriculum, and the most recent admissions notes.
areaDetail.q2: What fee range is common in {areaName}?
areaDetail.a2WithRange: Listed fee ranges in this area run from about Rp {minFee} to Rp {maxFee} per year, depending on program and age level.
areaDetail.a2: Fees vary by school and grade level. Open individual profiles and confirm the latest fee table with admissions.
areaDetail.q3: How should we shortlist schools in {areaName}?
areaDetail.a3: Start with commute time, then filter by curriculum and age range. After that, compare total first-year costs (tuition plus one-time fees).
areaDetail.q4: Can we visit or do a trial day?
areaDetail.a4: Many schools offer a campus tour and some offer trial days. Ask what your child can join and whether there is a trial fee.
areaDetail.q5: What should we confirm before enrolling?
areaDetail.a5: Confirm start dates, language support, class sizes, and what fees include (registration, uniforms, transport, meals, exams).

ageBand.a1: This page lists schools whose published age range overlaps roughly {min}–{max}. Confirm exact year levels, start dates, and placement with admissions.
ageBand.a2: Some schools accept transfers mid-year, others prefer term starts. Ask about space in your target grade and whether assessments are required.
ageBand.q3: What should we confirm for placement?
ageBand.a3: Confirm the child’s current grade, language level, and learning support needs. Ask how the school places new students and what documentation is needed.
ageBand.q4: What fees should we compare for this age band?
ageBand.a4: Ask for the total first-year cost: tuition plus registration, materials, uniforms, transport, meals, and any program fees that apply to this age group.
ageBand.a5: Pick a realistic commute, then shortlist 3–5 schools. Book tours close together and use the same question list so you can compare fairly.

budgetBand.a1: It’s a simple grouping based on annual tuition ranges. Exact fees vary by grade level and what’s included, so always request the current fee sheet from admissions.
budgetBand.q2: What costs are usually not included in tuition?
budgetBand.a2: Often: registration fees, uniforms, transport, meals, exams, some extracurriculars, learning materials, and capital levies. Ask for a full first‑year total.
budgetBand.q3: How do we shortlist within a budget band?
budgetBand.a3: Start with area (commute), confirm age/grade coverage, then compare curriculum and support needs. After that, ask each school for a breakdown of one‑time fees and monthly/annual extras.
budgetBand.q4: Do schools offer sibling discounts or payment plans?
budgetBand.a4: Some do. Ask whether there are sibling discounts, term-by-term payment options, and what happens if you start mid‑term.
budgetBand.a5: Schools can update fees each academic year and sometimes mid-year. Use this directory as a starting point and confirm details before enrolling.

curriculumDetail.q1: What does “{tagName}” mean for a school in Bali?
curriculumDetail.a1: Schools use curriculum labels in different ways. Open a profile and confirm the grade levels offered, the exam pathway (if any), and the language of instruction.
curriculumDetail.a2: Start with your area and commute, then confirm ages/grade levels and total first-year costs. A campus tour and a trial day (if offered) are usually the fastest way to sense fit.
curriculumDetail.q3: Do all {tagName} schools have the same fees?
curriculumDetail.a3: No. Fees vary by campus, facilities, and age level. Use the fee range shown on each profile as a starting point and confirm the latest fee table with admissions.
curriculumDetail.q4: Can we combine this curriculum with an area search?
curriculumDetail.a4: Yes. Browse by area first, then check which schools match {tagName}. For a tighter shortlist, pick the areas you’d actually commute from.

compareAreasDetail.a1: It depends on commute, your child’s age, and curriculum preference. Use this page to shortlist, then confirm availability and the latest fees with admissions.
compareAreasDetail.q2: How should we test commute time?
compareAreasDetail.a2: If possible, do a trial drive at drop‑off and pick‑up times. A route that looks short on a map can feel very different in school traffic.
compareAreasDetail.q3: What should we compare besides tuition?
compareAreasDetail.a3: Compare total first‑year cost (tuition + one‑time fees + recurring extras), language support, year levels offered, and how placement is handled for new students.
compareAreasDetail.a4: Yes — share your area preference, child’s age, and any curriculum needs. We’ll suggest a shortlist and a short question list to send to admissions.

curriculumsPage.title: Curriculums
curriculumsPage.subtitle: Explore schools by curriculum approach.

combine.title: Combine curriculum with area
combine.body: Curriculum is important, but daily commute is often the deciding factor. Pick an area first, then shortlist.

types.title: Browse by school type
types.body: Type labels can help if you’re looking for something specific (international, bilingual, Montessori, etc.).

fees.title: Fees and budget
fees.body: Compare schools using total first-year cost. Budget bands are a quick way to narrow your shortlist.

curriculumsPage.tip: Tip: You can combine curriculum with area and budget filters on the directory page.

feesPage.title: School fees in Bali
feesPage.subtitle: Fees are a moving target. This directory shows best-effort ranges as a starting point — then you confirm the latest fee sheet directly with admissions.

meaning.title: What the fee numbers usually mean
meaning.body: Many schools quote tuition per year. The part families miss is the “first-year total” — tuition plus one‑time registration, uniforms, transport, meals, exams, and activities.
meaning.listedBody: a fee range publicly available (still confirm the latest).
meaning.estimateBody: best-effort range based on available info; always request the school’s current fee table.

coverage.title: Directory coverage
coverage.body: Out of {total} schools in the directory, {listed} have publicly listed ranges and {estimate} are best-effort estimates.

budgetBand.title: Browse by budget band
budgetBand.body: Budget bands help you narrow quickly. Within any band, you’ll still see differences by age level and what’s included.

questions.title: Questions to ask every school
questions.q1: What is the total first-year cost (tuition + one-time fees)?
questions.q2: What is included (uniforms, meals, transport, books, exams, activities)?
questions.q3: Are there discounts (siblings, mid-year entry) or payment plans?
questions.q4: What refunds apply if plans change?
questions.q5: Are there additional support fees (EAL/learning support)?

shortlist.title: Prefer a short list?
shortlist.body: Tell us your area, your child’s age, and the curriculum you want. We’ll help you shortlist and prepare a simple script for admissions calls.

feesPage.disclaimer: Disclaimer: fee data changes frequently. Always confirm directly with the school.

areas.title: Common areas for this curriculum
areas.empty: Area breakdown isn’t available for these listings.
